skills: product intuition around prompts and automations
domain_experience: AI productivity content and audience development
assets: fast low-cost distribution for self-serve tests
ability to collect survey and usage data
distribution: strong reach to students, job-seekers, hobbyists, and ambitious individual AI users
weak direct reach to business buyers
network_access: consumer and prosumer AI productivity audience on X
constraints: does not currently have trust with business buyers
credibility_signals: large visible audience in the AI productivity category
unfair_advantages: can activate early self-serve users faster than a typical founder
can turn content distribution into product testing and data collection

binding_constraints: Current audience is real distribution but mostly to the wrong buyer for direct B2B sales
No service wedge means there is no fast path to borrowed B2B credibility
A durable product business is only credible if the first customer is an individual user and B2B trust is earned later

opportunities.title: Sponsor-funded AI productivity benchmark report
opportunities.thesis: Use the audience to collect benchmark data and sell sponsorship to AI vendors or adjacent tools.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The audience makes survey distribution and report launch easier than for a random operator.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Revenue is one-off and media-like, so it does not match the desired durable business.
validation_plan: Run one 20-question survey and target 300 responses from the existing audience
Pitch 10 potential sponsors and target 2 paid sponsor conversations within 7 days
kill_criteria: Fewer than 150 survey responses in 7 days
Zero sponsor calls booked after 10 targeted pitches

opportunities.title: AI workflow benchmark and certification product for teams
opportunities.thesis: Turn audience and product data into a benchmarking product for managers or L&D leaders.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The audience can seed data collection for a benchmark layer.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Managers may not trust the founder yet, so procurement and sales can stall.
validation_plan: Interview 10 managers or L&D leads about AI proficiency measurement
Test one benchmark concept page and target 5 demo requests
kill_criteria: Fewer than 3 qualified buyer conversations after 20 targeted outreaches
No buyer agrees the benchmark would influence a purchase or training decision

opportunities.title: Self-serve workflow operating system for ambitious AI users, then team analytics and controls
opportunities.thesis: Start with a self-serve product for ambitious individual users to save, run, and prove repeatable AI workflows, then add team analytics, shared libraries, and manager-facing controls once usage patterns appear.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The founder already has low-cost access to early adopters in AI productivity, so distribution works for a bottom-up product even though it does not yet work for enterprise sales.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If the product is too broad or does not anchor to one repeated job, it will look like a generic AI productivity wrapper and fail to retain users.
validation_plan: Pick one repeated job and launch one focused landing page
Target 150 visits, 30 email captures, and 10 qualified work-context respondents from 3 focused X posts
Interview 10 qualified users and target 6 who describe the same repeated workflow pain
Give 10 users prototype access and target 5 created workflows, 3 repeat runs, and 2 shares
Test willingness to pay with a preorder or beta commitment and target 5 preorders or 15 strong weekly-usage commitments
tool_specs: landing page for one workflow job
event tracking for repeat usage and shares
kill_criteria: Fewer than 30 qualified work-context signups from the first 3 focused posts
Fewer than 6 of 10 interviewed users describe the same repeated job
Fewer than 3 of 10 testers run a workflow more than once in 72 hours
Zero users prepay or make a strong weekly-usage commitment
Signups come mostly from curiosity users with no repeated work context

opportunities.title: Vertical partnership wedge inside career platforms or bootcamps
opportunities.thesis: Embed an AI workflow tool or benchmark into training platforms first, then expand toward employer-facing buyers.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Current audience and category credibility are closer to these channels than to enterprise procurement.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The business may get stuck in education-adjacent budgets instead of reaching durable business buyers.
validation_plan: Pitch 15 partner orgs and target 5 calls
Secure 1 pilot with at least 25 users
kill_criteria: Fewer than 3 partner calls after 15 targeted outreaches
No pilot partner accepts within 21 days

rejected_paths.title: Generic B2B AI copilot for companies
rejected_paths.reason: It matches the aspiration but not the real edge. There is no current buyer trust, buyer access, or service wedge to make enterprise adoption credible.

rejected_paths.title: Course or cohort business
rejected_paths.reason: It would monetize the current audience faster, but it conflicts with the stated goal of building a durable compounding business.

next_actions: Choose one repeated job with measurable time or quality improvement
Write a landing page with one promise and one hard metric
Ship a thin prototype with save, run, log, and share workflow actions
Run 3 focused X posts aimed at the chosen ICP instead of the whole audience
Interview 10 qualified users and measure repeat usage before expanding scope
